metadata.title: States PCS: Bihar GK for BPSC and other Bihar PCS Exams

questions.question: Recently Bihar got second place under the National MSME Award- 2022, Prime Minister Narendra Modi presented this award to Whom?

options.label: D
options.text: Sandeep Pondrick, Principal Secretary, Industries Department

questions.explanation: For the first time, Bihar has received the second prize in the National MSME Awards 2022 in the category of States and Union Territories. Sandeep Pondrick, Principal Secretary of, the Industries Department got this award from PM Narendra Modi. National MSME Awards are given in several categories for the outstanding work done by the state governments, institutions, or individuals for the rapid development and promotion of micro, small and medium scale across the country.

questions.question: The North Koel Canal connects Jharkhand to which district in Bihar?
questions.explanation: The North Koel Canal will connect Jharkhand to the Gaya district in Bihar. With an approved cost of ₹1,364 crore, it aims to enhance the irrigation abilities of the region, specifically in paddy and rabi crops. Not only does this benefit the local farmers, but it also strengthens the agricultural economy of Bihar. The Water Resources Department is managing the project, with work set to start in March or April 2023.

questions.question: What position did Bihar secure in the ‘National Water Award 2022’?
questions.explanation: Bihar secured the third position in the ‘National Water Award 2022’, an award organized by the Union Ministry of Jal Shakti. The award recognized Bihar for its demonstrated commitment to effective water conservation and resource management, notably through the Jal-Jeevan-Hariyali Abhiyan campaign. Bihar continues to align its initiatives with sustainable water management practices, alongside leading states in this area like Madhya Pradesh and Odisha.

questions.question: Bharat Singh Bharti, who received the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award, is known for his contribution to which art form in Bihar?
questions.explanation: Bharat Singh Bharti is known for his significant contribution to folk singing in Bihar. He has written and sung over 1,000 songs, including many Bhojpuri compositions, for which he received the prestigious Sangeet Natak Akademi Award. His lifelong dedication to preserving and promoting traditional art forms reflects the rich cultural heritage of Bihar.

questions.question: Who founded the Akhil Bharatiya Rashtravadi Kisan Sangathan in Bihar?
questions.explanation: Akhil Bharatiya Rashtravadi Kisan Sangathan was founded by Brahmeshwar Singh, known as the founder of Ranvir Sena, on 5 May 2012 in Patna, Bihar. It is an apolitical and non-violent organization advocating for the rights of farmers and manual workers.

questions.question: Which rock-cut cave is considered the earliest known example of the ogee-shaped chaitya arch in India?
questions.explanation: Lomas Rishi Cave, carved in the 3rd century BCE in Barabar Hills, Bihar, is the earliest example of an ogee-shaped chaitya arch, influencing later Buddhist cave architecture in western India.

questions.question: During its zenith, Nalanda Mahavihara was a renowned center for which branch of Buddhism?
questions.explanation: Nalanda Mahavihara, established in Bihar, flourished as a major center for Mahayana Buddhism, later accommodating Vajrayana studies. It attracted scholars from across Asia and held an extensive library. Supported by Indian and foreign rulers, its influence shaped Buddhist thought across the region for centuries.

questions.question: The Sultanganj Buddha, discovered in Bihar, belongs to which historical period?
questions.explanation: Sultanganj Buddha, a large copper statue found in Bihar, dates to the Gupta–Pala transitional period (c. 500–700 AD). Noted for its lost wax casting, abhayamudra posture, and Sarnath style robe, it is the largest known metal Buddha sculpture from ancient India.

questions.question: At which ancient site in Bihar is it believed that Mahākāśyapa, a disciple of Buddha, died while waiting for the future Buddha, Maitreya?
questions.explanation: Gurpa Hill, also called Kukkutapāda or Gurupādaka, is a major Buddhist site in Bihar, believed to be the death site of Mahākāśyapa. It is near Bodh Gaya, noted for its stupa and visited by Chinese traveler Xuanzang. It is one of the highest points in the Gangetic plains.

questions.question: Which community in Bihar celebrates the Ashtimki festival to commemorate the birth of Lord Krishna?
questions.explanation: Ashtimki is celebrated by the Tharu community of Bihar, coinciding with Krishna Janmashtami. The festival involves painting Ashtimki Chitra and marks the beginning of Harya Gurai. Tharus observe rituals and fasting, with ceremonies performed at the village elder’s residence.
